description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 7: The Cleanest Village (Addition, Subtraction, and Data Handling in Community Contexts)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent addition, subtraction, and data reading skills. Can fast-track through community math concepts with challenge content.

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to math in community and village life
Learn how to collect and organise village data
Practice addition and subtraction with village scenarios
Solve word problems using real village data
Fun wrap-up: adding, subtracting, and comparing data
Challenge: Apply all skills to complex village problems

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready. Provide complete coverage of community math, data collection, and problem solving.

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to math in community and village life
Learn data collection in village life
Wrap-up: adding, subtracting, and comparing village data

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in addition, subtraction, or data reading. Needs targeted review before community math concepts.

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Reading simple tables and lists
Start main topic: Math in the community

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in addition, subtraction, and data reading before attempting community math problems.

videoSequence.reason: Critical: Learn to read simple tables and lists
Gentle intro to math in village life
Begin main content: Data collection in villages

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with the village math chapter.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

reading_data.conceptName: Reading and understanding simple tables and lists

word_problems.conceptName: Solving simple word problems with addition and subtraction

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: A table shows the number of trees in three streets: Oak Street = 45, Pine Street = 32, Elm Street = 58. Which street has the most trees?
questions.explanation: Elm Street has 58 trees, which is more than 45 and 32. So Elm Street has the most trees!

questions.question: Why do we collect data in a village?
options: To understand what is happening and make good decisions
questions.explanation: We collect data to understand what is happening. For example, counting rubbish bags helps us know which areas need more cleaning!

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand data collection and reading tables well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with data reading, review tables and lists

checkpoints.checkpointName: Problem Solving with Village Data Check

questions.question: A village collected 325 bags of rubbish in week 1 and 287 bags in week 2. How many bags were collected in total?
questions.explanation: 325 + 287 = 612 bags in total. Ones: 5 + 7 = 12, write 2 carry 1. Tens: 2 + 8 + 1 = 11, write 1 carry 1. Hundreds: 3 + 2 + 1 = 6.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for the challenge wrap-up problems

ifWrong.message: Watch the village calculations video again, then try some practice problems with addition and subtraction.

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on addition and subtraction basics

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising addition and subtraction with real objects or village scenarios at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You are great at addition, subtraction, and reading data. You are ready to explore how maths is used in village life. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand addition, subtraction, and data reading well. Let's learn how maths is used in the cleanest village with complete coverage of all the topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in addition, subtraction, or reading data. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to village maths.

low_score.message: You need to review addition, subtraction, and reading tables first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn about the cleanest village.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how the village math concepts build on each other

concepts: Word problems with addition and subtraction
Complex word problems with real village data

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

additionSubtractionFluency.description: % of students who can solve 3-digit addition and subtraction within 30 seconds

dataReadingAccuracy.description: % of students who can correctly read and interpret simple tables

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 50-55 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (20 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 45-55 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students making carrying errors in addition with larger numbers
commonChallenges.solution1: Encourage writing numbers in columns and checking each place value. Use village scenarios to make practice meaningful.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students struggling to read data from tables and identify the right numbers
commonChallenges.solution2: Start with simple 2-column tables before moving to more complex ones. Use village cleaning data as a fun context.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students unsure whether to add or subtract in word problems
commonChallenges.solution3: Teach clue words: 'total', 'in all', 'altogether' mean add. 'Left', 'remaining', 'difference' mean subtract. Use village stories for practice.
